SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog

Class Session

java.lang.Object
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog.Session
public class Session extends Object
A borrowed handle to the DataFusion session available during a TableProvider.scanWithArgs(org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog.Session, org.apache.arrow.datafusion.catalog.ScanArgs) callback.

This provides access to session-level functionality like creating physical expressions from filters. It is NOT AutoCloseable because it does not own the underlying memory. It is only valid during the scan callback.

Example: 

@Override
public ExecutionPlan scanWithArgs(Session session, ScanArgs args) {
    if (args.filters() != null && !args.filters().isEmpty()) {
        PhysicalExpr expr = session.createPhysicalExpr(schema(), args.filters());
        // use expr for filter pushdown
    }
    return new MyExecutionPlan(schema());
}
See Also:

    createPhysicalExpr(org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema tableSchema, List<Expr> filters)

  • Constructor Details

    • Session

      public Session(org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Internal constructor. Users should not create Session instances directly.

  • Method Details

    • createPhysicalExpr

      public PhysicalExpr createPhysicalExpr(org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema tableSchema, List<Expr> filters)
      Creates a physical expression from the given filter expressions using a default session state.

      This conjoins all filter expressions with AND and compiles them into a physical expression using the provided table schema. The returned PhysicalExpr is owned and must be closed when no longer needed.

      Example: 

      PhysicalExpr expr = session.createPhysicalExpr(tableSchema, args.filters());
// use expr, then close when done
expr.close();
      Parameters:
      tableSchema - the schema of the table being scanned
      filters - the filter expressions to compile (borrowed, from the scan callback)
      Returns:
      a physical expression representing the conjunction of all filters
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if the physical expression cannot be created
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: Session::create_physical_expr
